meta.name: 10k Intermediate
meta.subtitle: Break 50 minutes over 16 weeks
meta.description: For runners with a solid 5k base who want to break 50 minutes for 10k. Sixteen weeks built around threshold running and progressive long runs, with a weekly track session for speed maintenance.
meta.coach_notes: Sub-50 for 10k requires running 5:00/km for 10km. The threshold runs on Thursday are the engine of this plan — they build your ability to hold that pace under fatigue. The long Sunday runs build the endurance to maintain it to the line.

weeks.title: Base assessment
weeks.note: First week. Tuesday is an assessment session — run the 1km reps at what feels like a controlled hard effort and note your splits. Thursday establishes your threshold feel. Do not force the paces — let this week tell you where you are.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 4x1km at current 10k pace (2.5min rec) · 2km CD · 10km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Pull-ups 2x12 · DB row 2x12 · Push-ups 2x12 · Face pulls 2x12 · Core circuit 2x12s — Your first upper body session. Focus on form, not load. Upper body and core strength directly improves your running posture and arm drive efficiency.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 20min threshold (5:05–5:15/km) · 2km CD · 8km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Pull-ups 2x12 · DB row 2x12 · Push-ups 2x12 · Face pulls 2x12 · Core circuit 2x12s — Your first upper body session. Focus on form, not load. Upper body and core strength directly improves your running posture and arm drive efficiency.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 7km easy · 5:50–6:15/km · Conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
10km easy · 5:55–6:20/km · Build endurance — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. 600ml water.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Building
weeks.note: Second week. The 800m reps introduce proper interval pace. The threshold run extends by two minutes. Both should feel demanding but controlled — you should finish each session having had more in the tank.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 6x800m at 10k pace (90sec rec) · 2km CD · 10km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Back squat 3x12 · Romanian deadlift 3x12 · Bulgarian split squat 3x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 3x12s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 22min threshold (5:02–5:12/km) · 2km CD · 8.5km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Pull-ups 3x12 · DB row 3x12 · Overhead press 3x12 · Face pulls 3x12 · Core circuit 3x12s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 7km easy · 5:50–6:15/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

11km easy · 5:55–6:20/km · Build endurance — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. 600ml water.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Volume peak
weeks.note: Highest volume week of the first block. Keep the easy sessions genuinely easy — Saturday and Sunday are aerobic maintenance, not training stimuli.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 5x1km at 10k pace (2min rec) · 2km CD · 11km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Pull-ups 3x12 · DB row 3x12 · Overhead press 3x12 · Face pulls 3x12 · Core circuit 3x12s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 25min threshold (5:00–5:10/km) · 2km CD · 9km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Back squat 3x12 · Romanian deadlift 3x12 · Bulgarian split squat 3x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 3x12s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:50–6:15/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

12km easy · 5:55–6:20/km · Build endurance — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. 600ml water.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Deload week
weeks.note: Deliberate recovery week. Volume drops by 35%. The hard sessions from the previous three weeks are being consolidated into fitness now. Do not add extra sessions — rest is the instruction.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 5km easy · Very relaxed — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. 600ml water.

weeks.title: Threshold development
weeks.note: Back to full intensity with a focus on threshold. The 2x12min blocks build sustainable race pace. If you struggled with the previous block's threshold runs, reduce pace by 5sec/km before building back up.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 8x400m at faster than 10k pace (75sec rec) · 2km CD · 9km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Pull-ups 3x12 · DB row 3x12 · Overhead press 3x12 · Face pulls 3x12 · Core circuit 3x12s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 2x12min threshold (5:00–5:10/km, 90sec rec) · 2km CD · 9km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

weeks.title: Race simulation
weeks.note: The Tuesday session includes a 3km time trial at race pace — the first time you run the actual speed you want to race at. 5:00/km should feel hard but achievable. If it feels impossible, adjust your goal.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 3km time trial at goal race pace (5:00/km) · 3x400m at faster pace (90sec rec) · 2km CD · 11km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Back squat 4x8 · Romanian deadlift 4x8 · Bulgarian split squat 4x8 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 4x8s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

weeks.note: Second volume peak. The long run reaches 14km — furthest of the plan. Keep it easy. Your legs should feel tired by Saturday, which is exactly right.

weeks.title: Speed sharpening
weeks.note: Phase 2. The interval pace gets faster — 200m reps at mile effort sharpen your neuromuscular system and make 5:00/km feel more comfortable by comparison.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 12x200m at mile effort (75sec rec) · 2km CD · 8km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

weeks.title: Race-pace intervals
weeks.note: The 1km reps at exact race pace are your most specific training session. These build confidence at 5:00/km under fatigue. Run rep 1 conservatively — you should be able to match it on rep 5.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 6x1km at exactly 5:00/km (2.5min rec) · 2km CD · 12km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Back squat 3x12 · Romanian deadlift 3x12 · Bulgarian split squat 3x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 3x12s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. 600ml water.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Peak week
weeks.note: Hardest week of the plan. The 5km race simulation on Tuesday is your dress rehearsal — run it at goal race pace and note how it feels. The 35-minute threshold on Thursday is the longest of the plan.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 5km at goal race pace (5:00/km) · 2km CD · 9km total — Threshold work at peak phase. Your lactate threshold determines the pace you can sustain for an hour. These sessions push that ceiling up.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 4x5 · Core circuit 4x5s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 35min continuous threshold (4:58–5:08/km) · 2km CD · 11km total — Threshold work at peak phase. Your lactate threshold determines the pace you can sustain for an hour. These sessions push that ceiling up.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 4x5 · Core circuit 4x5s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. 600ml water.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Taper prep
weeks.note: Volume starts coming down. Intensity stays. The 400m reps are fast and sharp — short enough to be manageable, fast enough to keep your neuromuscular system sharp.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 8x400m at faster than race pace (2min rec) · 2km CD · 9km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

weeks.title: Taper — week 1
weeks.note: Taper begins. Volume drops by 40%. Keep the intensity in your short sharp sessions. Your legs will feel odd mid-week — heavy, then light. This is normal taper adaptation.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 1.5km WU · 6x400m at race pace (2min rec) · 1.5km CD · 7km total — Sharp intervals close to race week keep your legs feeling quick without adding volume. The fitness is already built — this is a reminder, not a workout.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 15min threshold · 2km CD · 7km total — A short threshold session to maintain the feel of race effort. Comfortably hard — not racing.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. 600ml water.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Taper — week 2
weeks.note: Second taper week. Very light. By Thursday your legs should feel fresh and ready. If they feel too good, resist the urge to run harder — save everything for race day.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 1.5km WU · 4x400m at race pace (2min rec) · 1.5km CD · 5km total — Sharp intervals close to race week keep your legs feeling quick without adding volume. The fitness is already built — this is a reminder, not a workout.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 4km easy · Your legs should feel great — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

5km easy · Stay fresh for race week — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. 600ml water.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Race week prep
weeks.note: The week before race week. Stay calm, eat well, sleep well. No hard sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 4km easy · Strides optional — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

sessions.det: 3km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
4km easy · Arrive at race week completely fresh — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. 600ml water.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Race week
weeks.note: Race week. Everything serves Sunday's race. Rest, eat well, sleep well. The fitness is built — nothing you do this week will improve it. Your only job is to arrive fresh.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 3km easy · 4x100m strides at race pace · Light and sharp — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 3km easy · Very light · Just moving the legs — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is part of the training.

sessions.det: 2km easy · Stay loose — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

1km easy warm-up · 4x100m strides · Race: first km at 5:05, settle to 5:00, build final 3km · Trust the training — Race day. Trust your training. Start conservatively — most races are won or lost in the second half.

nut.d: Porridge with banana or toast with peanut butter. 400–500ml water. Only familiar foods — no experiments on race day.
150–200ml water. Light warm-up 10–15min easy. Trust your training.
Take water at stations. Sip, don't gulp. Consider a gel at halfway for 10-mile and longer distances if practised.
Water immediately. 400ml in 10 minutes. Keep moving to cool down.
Proper meal within 90 minutes. Protein, carbohydrates, and whatever you have been looking forward to. You earned this.
